stories.title: Electron-Ion Collider's radiofrequency controls system passes first real-world test
stories.desc: The U.S. Department of Energy's (DOE) Brookhaven National Laboratory has reached a key early milestone in developing radiofrequency control systems for the Electron-Ion Collider (EIC)—a next-generation research facility that will collide electrons with ions to reveal how the building blocks of…

stories.title: Investigating quantum and molecular plumbing in nanofluidics research
stories.desc: Our body contains an intricate system of tiny vessels through which blood, water and other molecules flow. When the size of the pipes shrinks to the nanoscale, where only a few molecules can fit side by side, the classical laws of physics governing the behavior of water are influenced by the atomic…

stories.title: Quantum mechanics theory may work without imaginary numbers, new analysis suggests
stories.desc: Physicists from Heinrich Heine University Düsseldorf (HHU) have examined a fundamental property of quantum mechanics in collaboration with the German Aerospace Center (DLR). In an article published in the journal Physical Review Letters, they show that this theory does not necessarily need to be…

stories.title: A Quiet Quantum Revolution in Earth’s Deep Interior
stories.desc: A subtle change in iron ions’ electronic configuration produces a measurable difference in seismic wave speeds through mantle rocks.

stories.title: Signs of Thaw in the Bering Sea
stories.desc: Drifting sea ice fragments near Alaska’s Saint Lawrence and Nunivak islands and colorful water around the Yukon Delta heralded the approach of the summer solstice. The post Signs of Thaw in the Bering Sea appeared first on NASA Science .
